title: The Quantum Hype Problem
summary: I work in quantum tech, and I'm asking you to be more skeptical of it. On marketing adjectives, timeline inflation, and why honesty is a survival strategy for the field.

title: Does the Machine Understand?
summary: Large language models write essays, prove lemmas, and pass exams. Whether they "understand" anything is a different question — and probably the wrong one.

title: Shut Up and Calculate?
summary: Physicists are told not to worry about what quantum mechanics means. But the history of the field suggests that worrying about it is exactly how progress gets made.

title: The Great Silence Is Data
summary: The Fermi paradox is better read as an observation than a paradox, and maybe the most important null result in science. On filters, boring explanations, and how seriously to take the silence.

title: The Media Skeptic's Guide to Health BS
summary: How to spot myths, misinformation, and marketing fluff in modern wellness culture — twelve persistent health myths, debunked.

title: Why Ethics Matters in Astrophysics
summary: The universe is a vast laboratory — but does that mean anything goes? On satellite constellations, sacred land, planetary protection, and who astronomy is for.

title: Is the Universe a Simulation?
summary: From Nick Bostrom's simulation argument to the limits of physical law — could reality be code?
